title: Animal Based Costco Haul for Less Than $15/Day

chunks.title: Introduction to Affordable Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The presenter addresses the misconception that an animal-based diet is too expensive. They aim to demonstrate how it can be maintained for under $15 a day, particularly at Costco.
chunks.transcript: I know what you guys think an animal- based diet is too expensive you're wrong I'm going to show you how to do animal based for less than $15 a day at Costco and just to prove my point I got the biggest thing you can use here to transport all your food I'm going to get the biggest things no I'm just joking I wanted to make it interesting in the video I got this big orange thing which I'm not going to take into the store I'm going to take one of the regular Costco carts in the store but yes eating meat eating fruit eating raw dairy or eating dairy eating honey this can be expensive high quality food costs more I think it's absolutely worth it but at a place like this you can do a very high quality animal-based diet for less than $15 a

chunks.title: Meat Selection and Pricing
chunks.summary: The presenter discusses the meat options available at Costco, particularly highlighting grass-fed Wagu beef and regular grass-fed beef, both priced at $5 per pound. They suggest that a daily intake of one pound would fit into the budget for the day.
chunks.transcript: right guys we're going to start with meat check this out Costco is actually pretty good for this you can get grass-fed wagu beef or regular grass-fed beef for $5 a pound so this is grass-fed beef this is wagu beef the wagu beef is going to be fattier they both say 100% grass-fed and it's only $5 a pound so that's pretty crazy let's say for your $15 a day animal- based diet you're going to eat a pound of meat per day we're at $5 so we're starting off right we got grass-fed beef we got a pound of grass-fed beef that is full of nutrients that's $5 out of our 15 to start and

chunks.title: Dairy Choices
chunks.summary: Costco offers multiple types of raw, unpasteurized cheeses. The presenter calculates the cost of incorporating cheese into the daily diet, estimating approximately $1.50 to $2.25 spent on cheese.
chunks.transcript: some Dairy so this is actually pretty cool guys Costco has not one not two not three four different cheeses that that are raw that are unpasteurized so most of the cheese here is pasteurized meaning the milk that you use to make the cheese has been heated it's been pasturized but they have four different types of cheese made from raw milk you've got parmesano regano peinao Romano this iiro cheese is raw and this guer from Switzerland is raw the peino guer and parmesano Gio are all about 75 cents per ounce so let's say you eat 2 oz of cheese per day that's $150 in cheese if you want to go crazy you want to eat 3 ounces of cheese per day you're at $225 in cheese per day in raw cheese some amazing cheese this iiro cheese is only about 50 cents per ounce so you could do 3 oz of this and be at $150 per

chunks.title: Inclusion of Fruits
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces fruits into the budget, starting with organic strawberries and blackberries. They outline how these fruits can be added without exceeding the $15 limit.
chunks.transcript: $6.50 or $7 25 into our $15 a day of animal based eating and we've got grass-fed burgers we've got raw cheese we're doing great let's keep going so let's find some fruit for our $15 a day or less animal-based eating Costco has organic strawberries for $3 a pound that's pretty great you could eat a pound of these strawberries a day for $3 which will take us to $10 25 cents a day for our animal-based eating and we're going to a little crazy we're going to add another fruit to that we're going to eat some blackberries over here these are $6 a pound you could eat a third of a pound of blackberries a day with your strawberries that is decadent and that'll be another $2 which will take us to

chunks.title: Adding Avocado and Honey
chunks.summary: Next, the presenter talks about including avocado and honey in the diet, demonstrating how these additions remain within the daily budget. They cleverly show how avocado can still be enjoyed as a daily indulgence.
chunks.transcript: eating an animal- based diet and we're not done yet let's go look at some bananas maybe we'll get an avocado we'll get some honey we're getting crazy all right I know you guys love avocados and you can even eat avocados for less than $15 a day on an animal- based diet here at Costco these avocados are $16 each you can decadently enjoy one entire avocado per day and you will only be at $13.81 give or take we still have more room we are still eating animal base for less than $15 a day we're not done yet let's go find some honey it's not in glass but it is shaped like a bear Costco has Organic Raw unfiltered honey it's a product of Brazil who knows if it contains glyphosate I don't know how close any pesticide containing Farms are to the hives but it's Organic Raw and unfiltered pretty good wish it were in glass but it's 17 cents an ounce an ounce is about 2 tablespoons of honey so you want to have two tablespoons of honey per day in your tea or your coffee I don't drink tea or coffee but you want to have some honey in your tea or coffee or you want to have honey post-workout you want to have honey with some raw milk you can get two tablespoons a day for 17 cents we're not even at $14 a day

chunks.title: Final Budget Item: Yogurt
chunks.summary: The presenter wraps up the selection with A2 organic whole milk yogurt, explaining its benefits and pricing. With this final item, they confirm that all selections fit within the $15 budget.
chunks.transcript: spend this last dollar of our $15 a day animal- based diet let's go all right we've got $1 per day left and I think I'm going to spend it on some yogurt this stuff is pretty cool it is not raw I would prefer a raw yogurt but Costco has A2 organic whole milk yogurt A1 versus A2 refers to the type of casine protein in the milk a lot of people tolerate A2 better A2 is from Jersey cows they say this is grass-fed and they say this is from Jersey cows so this is an A2 probably more digestible yogurt and it has 4.5% milk fat that's where the good stuff is I don't fear that this is 15 cents per ounce so you can eat8 ounces which is more than a cup of yogurt per day and still be eating an animal based diet for $15 a day how

chunks.title: Conclusion and Recap
chunks.summary: The presenter concludes by summarizing the foods acquired for under $15 a day, emphasizing the quality of the items and encouraging viewers that a nutritious animal-based diet is achievable on a budget.
chunks.transcript: in the pudding baby proof is in the yogurt let's go we're going to go through this again of course I'm going to park right next to the soybean oil and corn oil this is giving me a little PTSD behind me but we just shopped at Costco for less than $15 a day my friends for an animal-based diet and this is not a Shabby animal based diet this is grass-fed beef this is organic fruit this is raw cheese this is raw organic honey and this is organic A2 grass-fed yogurt I think that's pretty good and it's still less than $15 a day hopefully this video is helpful see you
